message:
feat(fcp-registry): add NoTrustedSignature error variant and improve publisher verification error cascading

Introduces a dedicated `RegistryError::NoTrustedSignature` variant to
distinguish the case where *no* publisher or registry signature verified
from the more specific `RegistrySignatureRequired` (which implies a
registry signature was expected but absent).  The previous code path
mapped both situations onto `RegistrySignatureRequired`, losing the
diagnostic signal needed to differentiate policy misconfiguration from
missing registry endorsement.

Publisher verification (`verify_publishers`) now:
- Returns `PublisherThresholdUnmet` immediately when the publisher list
  is empty but a non-zero threshold is declared, instead of silently
  returning `Ok(false)`.
- Preserves the first concrete verification error (e.g. UnknownKid,
  SignatureInvalid) encountered while iterating signatures, and surfaces
  it when no signature validates at all — giving operators an actionable
  root cause rather than a generic threshold failure.

Test coverage:
- Unit: empty-publisher-with-threshold threshold check, empty-signature-
  section NoTrustedSignature path, display round-trip and exhaustiveness
  vectors.
- Integration: end-to-end bundle verification for both threshold and
  no-trusted-signature failure modes without mocks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
